data.title: Xenon joins Russell 3000 and Russell 2000 indexes
data.description:
• Xenon Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:XENE) on Friday said the company has been added to the broad-market Russell 3000 and small-cap Russell 2000 Indexes as part of the annual reconstitution, effective at the close of U.S. equity markets on Friday, June 27, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: MercadoLibre snaps six days of gains as shares trade in red
data.description:
MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI) shares snapped six straight days of gains as the stock closed 0.01% lower, at $2560.05 on Friday. 

The online marketplace company gained more than 6% in the last six trading days. The stock has gained more than 51% so far this year, compared to an over 4% gain in the broader benchmark index. 

MELI is down 1% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.78% higher, at $2560.20 on Thursday. 

Shares of MercadoLibre have been under pressure since early this month after the e-commerce giant said it lowered the threshold for free shipping in Brazil to compete with rival Amazon in the Latin American market.

The company will now offer free shipping on orders of $3.40 or more from the prior minimum of $14.15.

According to Bank of America, MercadoLibre's actions to reduce seller shipping fees and offer wider free-shipping should attract more buyers and sellers to the platform, as well as increase the overall level of inventory.

Analyst Robert Aguilar said an increase in buying frequency and more trials of new categories could MercadoLibre's gross merchandise volume and increase route density, which would also push margins higher.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system has rated the stock HOLD, while SA authors and sell-side analysts have rated the stock BUY.

data.title: Micron 10-Q reveals one customer accounted for 16% of revenue: Wells Fargo
data.description:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) recently filed its 10-Q for its fiscal third-quarter, and Wells Fargo pointed out that one customer accounted for a significant portion of its revenue. 

“MU's 10-Q discloses that it had one 10%+ customer during the 9-mos ending F3Q25—accounting for 16% of cumulative [9-month ended May revenue],” analyst Aaron Rakers wrote in a note to clients. “This compares to a disclosed ~15% customer for 1HFY25. Assuming this is the same customer, this would imply ~$1.7 billion in F3Q25 revenue, vs. our estimate of ~$1.1B and ~ $1.4B in F1Q25 and F2Q25.”

The filing adds that the unnamed customer is primarily included in Micron's Compute and Networking Business Unit. This implies that the customer accounts for roughly 25% to 30% of Micron's Compute and Networking Business Unit revenue, Rakers added.

Other notable takeaways from the 10-Q include a decline in customer prepays, as contract liabilities from customer prepayments fell to around $146M at the end of the quarter, compared to $614M and $555M in the first and second quarters; and the receipt of $266M in government incentives to offset capex, compared to $963M in the first quarter of fiscal 2025 and $33M in the year-ago quarter.

“The company's liabilities include a $603M balance in non-current unearned [government] incentives vs. $836M in the prior [quarter],” Rakers added.

data.title: Cineverse outlines multi-film release slate and targets AI-driven growth following 58% revenue increase
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cineverse Corp. (CNVS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Christopher J. McGurk highlighted that the fourth quarter continued "very strong financial and business momentum," with total revenue of $15.6 million and net income of $858,000, both representing considerable increases over the prior year. He stressed, "we've continued that very strong financial and business momentum in our fourth fiscal quarter, generating impressive growth in all our financial performance measures and beating consensus analyst guidance on all key metrics."
  • McGurk emphasized the strategic reorganization, stating the company has "set up our technology business as a separate business group and put Tony Huidor in charge as President and Chief Product Officer" to capitalize on proprietary streaming content management and AI technology. He also introduced the creation of a dedicated Theatrical Motion Pictures division, led by Yolanda Macias as Chief Motion Pictures Officer, focusing on building a wide release slate modeled after the successful Terrifier franchise.
  • The next wide release, The Toxic Avenger, is set for August 29. McGurk explained the company’s investment in each upcoming film will be "less than $5 million," and "the film will generate approximately the same level of financial return to Cineverse as Terrifier 3 if it performs at only half of Terrifier's box office level."
  • CFO Mark Wayne Lindsey stated, "We are able to beat analyst consensus estimates for revenue, net income, diluted EPS and adjusted EBITDA for both the fourth quarter and the full year." He reported revenues of $15.6 million for the quarter, net income of $0.9 million, and adjusted EBITDA of $4 million, calling attention to a direct operating margin of 55%. SG&A expenses fell to $5.4 million, or 35% of revenues. "We had $13.9 million in cash and cash equivalents on our balance sheet as of March 31, 2025 with $0 outstanding on our $12.5 million working capital facility."
  • President and Chief Strategy Officer Erick Opeka noted that Screambox subscribers grew 31% since the Home Premier of Terrifier 3, and the streaming business saw "over 3.2 billion minutes streamed across our owned and operated services, up 45% over the prior year."

Outlook

  • Management plans at least three wide release films in the current fiscal year, each with investments below $5 million and with a focus on franchise IP properties. Upcoming releases include The Toxic Avenger, Silent Night, Deadly Night, and Return to Silent Hill.
  • McGurk stated, "Expect more wide release film announcements in the next few months as we build our slate for fiscal year 2026."
  • CFO Lindsey expects direct operating margin "to remain in the 45% to 50% range" for future quarters and anticipates SG&A expenses will continue to decline as a percentage of revenues.
  • Opeka said the company plans to expand the new Cineverse streaming channel beyond Amazon and will "focus our investments in growth on these 4 properties in the short to midterm"—Screambox, Dove, Fandor, and Midnight Pulp.

Financial Results

  • The fourth quarter reported revenue was $15.6 million, with net income of $0.9 million and adjusted EBITDA of $4 million. The direct operating margin was 55%, above the company's target range.
  • SG&A expenses decreased to $5.4 million, or 35% of revenues. Cash and cash equivalents stood at $13.9 million as of March 31, 2025, with $0 outstanding on the $12.5 million working capital facility and a working capital surplus of $3.6 million.
  • For the year, total revenues grew to $78.2 million, net income reached $3.8 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $13.9 million. Net cash provided by operations was $18.5 million for the year.

Q&A

  • Daniel Louis Kurnos, The Benchmark Company: "Chris, high level, just if you guys are successful or see early signs of success, how much more are you willing to lean in?" CEO McGurk responded that more films following the Terrifier blueprint would be announced, with expansions into family, fantasy, Black Cinema, and comedy. "Once we put those pieces into our release slate, I think that's when we'll get really serious about negotiating a pay deal."
  • Kurnos also asked about operating margin potential. CFO Lindsey replied, "margin last quarter was 49%." McGurk added, "we feel good about the 55% number that we put up, and we feel really good that we'll meet or exceed our operating margin target of 45% to 50% going forward."
  • Brian David Kinstlinger, Alliance Global Partners, inquired about the pipeline and deal sizes for Matchpoint and cineSearch. Mark Antonio Huidor indicated that "each of those studios is probably $5 million and up...So that $5 million can easily scale much higher." Opeka added, "the reception has been very, very robust."
  • Kinstlinger asked about podcast monetization. Opeka stated, "podcast CPMs, higher than CTV CPMs, sometimes by even $10 or more higher on a direct basis" and that average deal sizes are "low 6 figures per deal."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were broadly positive, focusing on the company’s slate expansion, technology pipeline, and monetization strategies. Their questions highlighted interest in scalability, profitability, and new business lines.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, repeatedly referencing strong performance and future opportunities. McGurk emphasized, "we feel really good that we'll meet or exceed our operating margin target."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts exhibited similarly optimistic sentiment, though this quarter saw increased emphasis on AI and technology-driven growth opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, with management reiterating operating margin targets and the focus on cost control. The strategic focus shifted toward the creation of dedicated entertainment and technology divisions, with a new emphasis on AI and the Matchpoint platform.
  • The prior quarter emphasized rapid growth from the Terrifier 3 release, while the current quarter broadened the focus to a diversified slate and technology commercialization.
  • Key metrics such as revenue, net income, and margin continued to show improvement, though at lower levels due to seasonality as outlined by management.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from individual film economics to scalability of new technology products and recurring revenue streams.
  • Management’s confidence in the sustainability of margins and future revenue growth was sustained and reinforced.

Risks and Concerns

  • CFO Lindsey noted, "the depressed direct and programmatic advertising environment in the first quarter, which is a direct result of companies pulling back on their discretionary advertising spend due to the ever-changing tariff environment."
  • Opeka described a "glut of supply with huge growth in available competitive channels from studios outstripping the audience growth in the market," which has put pressure on CPMs and fill rates for programmatic advertising.
  • Huidor noted that major studios "still rely on manual workflows, fragmented system, a patchwork of external vendors and massive internal teams," which presents both a challenge and opportunity for Matchpoint.

Final Takeaway

Cineverse Corp. delivered another quarter of strong revenue and margin growth, supported by a diversified film slate, record streaming engagement, and a newly structured focus on technology and AI. The company’s outlook is anchored by upcoming wide releases, robust technology commercialization opportunities with Matchpoint and cineSearch, and continued cost discipline. Management is confident in its ability to sustain high margins, expand its addressable market, and drive long-term value through strategic investments in content and technology.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Jobs Report, Tesla deliveries, Constellation Brands earnings, and Circle ratings
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - June 30

  • Volatility watch - SharpLink Gaming (SBET) and INmune Bio (INMB) have a higher level of implied volatility than normal based on options trading. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Anbio Biotechnology (NNNN), Belo Sun Mining (OTCQB:BSXGF), and Citius Oncology (CTOR). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include beer stocks Molson Coors (TAP) and Boston Beer (SAM).
  • IPO watch - The quiet period expires on Circle Internet Group (CRCL) and Omada Health (OMDA) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Progress Software (PRGS).
  • Dividend watch - Some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Deere & Company (DE), Heico (HEI), Cisco (CSCO), and Progressive (PGR).
  • All day - Ralliant (RAL) will begin trading after being spun off from Fortive (FTV). Ralliant was formed from Fortive's Precision Technologies segment, which has a legacy of innovation in test and measurement systems, specialty sensors, and aerospace, defense, and space subsystems. Fortive will concentrate on Intelligent Operating Solutions and Advanced Healthcare Solutions, while Ralliant will focus exclusively on precision technologies.
  • 10:00 a.m. Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will speak on the economic outlook and monetary policy before an MNI Livestreamed Connect Video Conference.

 

Tuesday - July 1

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Constellation Brands (STZ) and Greenbrier Companies (GBX). 
  • Data watch - Key data reports due to arrive during the first few days of the week include updates on deliveries from Tesla (TSLA), Nio (NIO), XPeng (XPEV), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Li Auto (LI), ZEEKR Intelligent (ZK), Polestar Automotive (PSNY), and Lucid Group (LCID). Quarterly sales reports from General Motors (GM), Ford Motor (F), and Toyota (TM) will also be closely watched. Other reports of high interest to investors will be the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release, the monthly update on Class 8 truck orders, firearm background check data from the FBI, and discount broker DARTs reports.
  • Central bank watch: The European Central Bank's forum on central banking in Sintra will include a policy panel with the heads of the Federal Reserve, the ECB, the Bank of Japan, the Bank of England, and the Bank of Korea.
  • 9:30 a.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will participate in a policy panel before the European Central Bank Forum on Central Banking 2025, "Adapting to Change: Macroeconomic Shifts and Policy Responses."
  • 10:00 a.m. The June Construction Spending report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.

 

Wednesday - July 2

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include UniFirst (UNF).
  • All day - Universal Pictures' (CMCSA) Jurassic World Rebirth will open in the U.S. The film is expected to generate between $115 million and $135 million in its opening weekend in the U.S.
  • All day - It is the five-year anniversary since Lemonade's (LMND) IPO.

 

Thursday - July 3

  • 7:30 a.m. The European Central Bank will release the minutes from its last policy meeting.
  • 8:30 a.m. The June U.S. Jobs Report will be released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics. Economists expect job additions to fall to 120K from 139K in May and the unemployment rate to increase to 4.3%.
  • 10:00 a.m. Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will give a keynote speech on U.S. monetary policy in the IMFS Distinguished Lecture Series hosted by Institute for Monetary and Financial Stability.

 

Friday - July 4

  • All day - U.S. stock markets will be closed for observance of the Independence Day holiday.

data.title: Visa, Mastercard merchant fees violate antitrust law, U.K. court rules
data.description:
Visa (NYSE:V) and Mastercard's (NYSE:MA) default multilateral interchange fees came under fire Friday after London's Competition Appeal Tribunal ruled they violate European antitrust law, in a big win for the hundreds of merchants who filed complaints about such "excessive" fees.

The tribunal held the liability phase of the case early last year. A separate decision on whether retailers passed any of the alleged excess charges on to consumers is still to come.

V and MA's multilateral interchange fees are charges that stores have to pay to card-issuing banks every time a customer transacts.

The payment network giants disagreed with the U.K. court's decision and planned to seek permission to appeal, Reuters reported, citing company spokespeople.

The merchants were represented by law firm Scott + Scott in the case.

Meanwhile, in midafternoon trading, shares of MA and V gained 1% and 0.9%, respectively.

data.title: Wall Street is mixed after record highs as Trump ends trade talks with Canada
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages are mixed on Friday after both the S&P 500 (SP500) and Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) notched fresh all-time trading highs.

The benchmark S&P 500 S&P 500 (SP500) is near flat, the blue-chip the Dow (DJI) has advanced 0.5%, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) is slipped by 0.2%.

U.S. Treasury yields inched up across the curve with the shorter-end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) moving up 2 basis points to 3.74% while the longer-end U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) has pushed up 4 basis points to 4.28%.

Looking out at how each S&P sector is performing and it can be observed that eight of the 11 segments are in positive territory as Industrials leads the way. At the other end of the spectrum Energy has suffered the most so far. 

On the economic front, May's core PCE Price Index came in at +0.2% M/M versus the +0.1% consensus and +0.1% prior (unrevised) level, and +2.7% Y/Y compared to the +2.6% consensus and +2.6% prior (revised from +2.5%). 

PCE Price Index: +0.1% M/M vs. +0.1% consensus and +0.1% prior (unrevised), and +2.3% Y/Y vs. +2.3% consensus and +2.2% prior (revised from +2.1%). 

Also the U.S. consumer sentiment gauge came in at 60.7 in June's final estimate, compared with the mid-month reading of 60.5 and May's 52.2, according to data released by the University of Michigan on Friday.

Moreover, on the geopolitical trade front, the U.S. and China finalized a trade deal. The White House also noted that it has imminent plans to reach agreements with 10 major trading partners. 

Additionally, U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday said he was ending all trade discussions with Canada, effective immediately, after claiming that the country had imposed a digital services tax on U.S. technology companies.

“Yesterday President Trump confirmed that the US and China have formally signed a new trade agreement, bringing months of negotiations to a close. The deal cements the framework established in recent Geneva meetings, transforming the previous handshake consensus into a binding agreement. While details remain sparse, the announcement removed another layer of uncertainty from the global risk environment. Investors welcomed the confirmation as a positive signal for supply chains and global trade, even if the implementation timeline remains vague,” Trade Nation's David Morrison said.

As for stocks that are on the move, shares of Nike (NKE) have popped up by 15.1% as Wall Street sees early strides in the company’s turnaround — from trimming bloated inventory and tightening supply chain gaps to doubling down on its core growth engines: running and basketball.

data.title: Trump says ending all trade talks with Canada
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday said he was ending all trade discussions with Canada, effective immediately, after claiming that the country had imposed a digital services tax on U.S. technology companies. 

Wall Street pared some gains after Trump's post on his Truth Social service. Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

And here are some Canada-linked ETFs:  (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FXC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA), and (BATS:BBCA).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

This is a developing story, please check back for updates

data.title: SA Asks: What are the best positioned shipping stocks right now?
data.description:
Which shipping stocks are best positioned to navigate the Middle East crisis?

Seeking Alpha analysts Soroya Investments and Melissa Tucker offer their thoughts. 

Soroya Investments: Threats of closing the Hormuz strait and the jamming of GPS signals disrupt the supply side, causing fewer ships to be available. VLCC [Very Large Crude Carrier] rates have increased dramatically recently. Tankers and gas carriers have already benefited from this imbalance. 

Well-positioned actors include Frontline (NYSE:FRO) and BW LPG (NYSE:BWLP) (OTCPK:BWLLY). The low order book-to-fleet ratio also aids the former. Besides, China relies on Iranian imports for nearly one-eighth of its oil and will lose heavily if the Hormuz strait were to close. They will not allow that to happen. Even though active warfare between Iran and Israel seems to have paused, uncertainty remains.

Melissa Tucker: VLGC [Very Large Gas Carrier] stocks such as Dorian LPG (NYSE:LPG) and BW LPG (NYSE:BWLP) are the best positioned to benefit from the Middle East crisis. VLGC rates were already trending higher before the Iranian crisis, and Iran exports about 7.5% of global volumes through short-haul trade to India and China. 

Recent attacks on refineries will reduce the amount Iran can export, and those volumes could be compensated with higher U.S. exports, increasing ton miles. Moreover, congestion in the Hormuz Strait could increase inefficiencies and rates. There is no need for further escalation for those stocks to shine, as the damage to the refineries is already done and will take several months to repair. Even with the ceasefire, rates haven’t tumbled.

  • Top Oil and Gas Storage and Transportation Stocks
  • Top Marine Transportation Stocks

data.title: Campbell's Q3 beat overshadowed by cautious forecast and GIS fallout
data.description:
Weighed down by General Mills’ (GIS) gloomy outlook and the company’s own conservative profit outlook for FY25, shares of The Campbell’s Company (NASDAQ:CPB) set a new low on Friday after a three-day losing streak.

Campbell’s (NASDAQ:CPB) FQ3 results  exceeded expectations, with a 4% improvement in sales and 2 cent erosion in profit. But the company’s profit margin was squeezed by 110 basis points, and FY25 adjusted earnings are now expected to be at the low end of its guidance range, excluding any negative impact from tariffs. Campbell’s (NASDAQ:CPB) targets a $0.03 to $0.05 tariff headwind to earnings, which was not factored into its EPS target of $2.95 to $3.05 versus $2.98 estimates.

Campbell’s (CPB) shares were pressured, however, by a gloomy assessment of the consumer environment from packaged food peer General Mills (GIS) earlier this week.

The company said it expects net sales to be down 1% to up 1% this year and a 10% to 15% drop in adjusted operating profit and EPS.

But while the negative reaction in General Mills’ (GIS) stock could be attributed more to idiosyncratic factors rather than sector issues in the broader consumer market, shares within the packaged food sector all came under pressure in sympathy, including Campbell’s (CPB).

At $30.81 per share, Campbell’s shares are flashing oversold. Short interest of 6.41% is comparable to other packaged food names, including General Mills (GIS), Kraft Heinz (KHC), and Post (POST).
